title: jasminum arborescens var. roxburghianum
intro: Jasminum arborescens var. roxburghianum, also known as the Roxburgh's star jasmine, is a fragrant and flowering plant native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The Roxburgh's star jasmine is believed to offer various health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, skin problems, and even certain types of cancer. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils that are used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
